reasons: Complete executable implementation with detect_intraday_anomaly_revised.py containing full surge and V-reversal detection logic
Correctly implements 30-minute K-line approach as specified in requirements (not daily approximation)
Provides concrete evidence by referencing 3 actual detected signals from GA's real mootdx data (000669, 000793, 000795)
Proper volume verification logic: checks if anomaly volume > 5x average of prior periods
Implements next-day continuation verification: checks if next day close >= anomaly day low
Output file satisfies contract with proper format and clear documentation of data limitations
Superior to ClaudeCode which used daily K-line approximation instead of required 30-min data
Superior to GA which provided no executable code artifacts despite documenting limitations well
OpenClaw completely failed with concurrency error and produced no output file
merge_notes: Codex provides the most complete solution with executable code implementing the exact task requirements. While all agents faced 2024-08-22 historical data limitations, Codex distinguished itself by: (1) implementing the full 30-minute K-line detection logic as specified, (2) providing working Python code that can be reused, and (3) documenting concrete signal examples from peer agent's real data. ClaudeCode's daily K-line approximation is a reasonable fallback but deviates from the core requirement. GA documented constraints well but lacked executable artifacts. The confidence is 0.85 rather than higher because all agents struggled with historical data access, but Codex made the best of the situation by providing reusable implementation and referencing actual results.
